title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition
content:
Charge-Offs & Recoveries
Year-to-date gross charge-offs totaled $911,000.
Losses remain concentrated in New Vehicle ($336,000), Used Vehicle ($307,000), and Indirect Loans ($493,000).
Recovery data was not disclosed in the source materials for this period,

Allowance & CECL Position
The CECL allowance decreased to $2.985 million, reflecting a 13.4% decrease from the previous quarter.
Reserve levels remain aligned with current portfolio risk characteristics, declining charge-off activity, and the decline in overall loan balances.
Reserve assumptions continue to incorporate both historical loss experience and forward-looking economic expectations.

Management continues to evaluate CECL assumptions and loss factors to ensure reserve adequacy remains appropriate for the evolving risk profile of the loan portfolio.
Overall Assessment
Loan performance reflects manageable credit risk despite continued contraction in loan balances. Delinquencies remain concentrated within New Vehicle, Used Vehicle, Indirect Loans, All Other Secured Non-Real Estate Loans/Lines of Credit, and Whole or Partial Loans Purchased/Secured by Junior Lien on a Single 1-to 4-Family Residential Property segments, while charge-off activity has declined compared with prior periods. The portfolio continues to be supported by adequate capital and appropriate reserve coverage.
Management remains focused on disciplined underwriting, proactive collection efforts, and ongoing portfolio monitoring to preserve asset quality while supporting future loan growth and earnings stability.
